content: We can reframe unsupervised graph anomaly detection as a curiosity-driven reinforcement learning task, where an ICM-augmented autonomous agent is rewarded for intrinsically exploring under-sampled anomalous subgraphs in diverse real-world wild graph data, rather than relying on static generative modeling to score anomalies. This replaces the common "fit the normal graph distribution" paradigm with an active search paradigm where the agent's intrinsic reward for novel subgraph structures naturally surfaces rare anomalies that match the real-world diversity of data in UniWildGraph.
